title: Meet Our Team
content: Building the future of Python education through expertise, experience, and community At Real Python, our team combines decades of real-world Python development experience with proven educational expertise. From Fortune 500 companies to open-source projects, our team members bring practical knowledge directly from the industry trenches to create content that actually works “in production.” More about our origin story → Dan Bader Dan is the owner and editor-in-chief of Real Python and the main developer of the realpython.com learning platform. Dan has been writing code for more than 20 years and holds a master's degree in computer science. Sarah Jane Ross Sarah Jane manages content operations at Real Python, leveraging more than ten years of remote-workflow expertise to refine metrics, SEO, and processes that support the team in delivering high-quality Python education. Martin Breuss Martin is Real Python's Head of Content Strategy. With a background in education, he's worked as a coding mentor, code reviewer, curriculum developer, bootcamp instructor, and instructional designer. Our editors ensure every piece of content meets the highest standards for accuracy, clarity, and practical application. Brenda Weleschuk Brenda refines content for a great learning experience as Real Python's Editor. With a background in technical writing, teaching, and instructional design, she brings a sharp eye to any learning resource. Tappan Moore Tappan is the Video Editor for Real Python: he cleans up each course's audio & video, and adds graphical spice. Kate Finegan Kate relishes a well-placed comma as Tutorial Editor for Real Python. Our staff educators combine deep technical knowledge with proven teaching ability to make complex concepts accessible. Christopher Bailey Christopher Bailey is the creator and host of the Real Python podcast. He also manages the video course pipeline, coordinating with the video course creators and our in house editor. He is a programmer and data analyst. Philipp Acsany Philipp is a core member of the Real Python team. He creates tutorials, records video courses, and hosts Office Hours sessions to support your journey to becoming a skilled and fulfilled Python developer. Bartosz Zaczyński Bartosz is an experienced software engineer and Python educator with an M.Sc. in Applied Computer Science. Leodanis Pozo Ramos Leodanis is a self-taught Python developer, educator, and technical writer with over 10 years of experience. Stephen Gruppetta Stephen obtained a PhD in physics and worked as a physicist in academia for over a decade before becoming a Python educator. He's constantly looking for simple ways to explain complex things in Python. Christopher Trudeau Christopher has a passion for the Python language and writes, records, and podcasts for Real Python. He is a consultant who helps advise organizations on how to improve their technical teams. Aldren Santos Aldren Santos holds a degree in Multimedia Arts and Sciences from Mapúa University and brings Python tutorials to life with original artwork across Real Python. His illustrations make learning more visual and approachable. Andres Pineda Andres is Real Python's Community Manager. He has a B.Sc. in Computer Science and over 15 years of experience building software for various industries. Andres has spoken at PyCon several times and is a contributing organizer at PyCascades. Alexandra Davis Alexandra is an avid Pythonista and course instructor at Real Python based in Arizona. She is a software engineer with a passion for full stack web development and Python. Bruce Benson Bruce is Real Python's Sponsorships and Sales Director. Get in touch with him to learn about our Sponsorship & Advertising programs or to explore Real Python for Teams corporate training options. Our content library is enriched by a network of Python professionals who contribute their expertise on a project basis. These contributors bring specialized knowledge from a broad set of industries and backgrounds, ensuring our content covers the full spectrum of Python applications. We’re grateful to work with over 190 contributors who have shared their expertise with our community: Show all team members and contributors → Interested in contributing? We’re frequently looking for experienced Python professionals who are passionate about education and sharing knowledge. View open positions →
